title: Review this uncommitted diff for bugs, security issues, and code quality. Be concise (max 10 lines).
summary_text: - High: `[CLAUDE.md:180](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L180)` says `Manus` or `NB2` is allowed, but `[CLAUDE.md:242](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L242)` says `Manus` only and `[CLAUDE.md:244](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L244)` says Gemini should not be used. This is an operational contradiction that will produce inconsistent human/agent behavior. - High: `[CLAUDE.md:242](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/CLAUDE.md#L242)` documents delegation to `delegate-manus.js`, but that script is not present in the repo. The new “single path” instruction is not implementable as written. - Medium: Runtime policy still disagrees with the doc: `[account_profile.py:16](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/account_profile.py#L16)` and `[account_profile.py:18](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/account_profile.py#L18)` still instruct NB2/Excalidraw and even HTML fallback. Changing only `CLAUDE.md` will not change actual generation behavior. - No direct security issue in this diff; main risk is policy/runtime drift causing wrong tool selection or blocked image generation.
